with:
  # Mapping of predicates to human‑readable phrase generators.
      def get_phrase(predicate: str, obj: str) -> str:
          """Generate a readable phrase from predicate and object."""
          # Airport-specific predicates
          if predicate == "cityServed":
              return f"serves the city of {obj}"
          elif predicate == "elevationAboveTheSeaLevel":
              if "metre" in str(obj).lower() or "meter" in str(obj).lower() or str(obj).isdigit():
                  return f"is at an elevation of {obj} metres above sea level"
              return f"is at an elevation of {obj} above sea level"
          elif predicate == "elevationAboveTheSeaLevelInFeet":
              return f"is at an elevation of {obj} feet above sea level"
          elif predicate == "elevationAboveTheSeaLevelInMetres":
              return f"is at an elevation of {obj} metres above sea level"
          elif predicate == "location":
              return f"is located in {obj}"
          elif predicate == "operatingOrganisation":
              return f"is operated by {obj}"
          elif predicate == "runwayLength":
              if "metre" in str(obj).lower() or str(obj).isdigit():
                  return f"has a runway length of {obj} metres"
              return f"has a runway length of {obj}"
          elif predicate == "runwayName":
              return f"has a runway named {obj}"
          elif predicate == "country":
              return f"is in {obj}"
          elif predicate == "isPartOf":
              return f"is part of {obj}"
          elif predicate.endswith("RunwayLengthFeet"):
              rn = predicate.split("Runway")[0].lower()
  ... (88 more lines)
